verificationNote: E-commerce cloud security requirements constructed from PCI DSS v4.0, Malaysia PDPA 2010, BNM e-money guidelines, and cloud security best practices. These are indicative -- verify against current PCI SSC guidance and Malaysian regulatory directives.
nacsaSectorLead: Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living / MDEC
nacsaSectorNumber: Subset of Trade, Industry and Economy sector (sector 10)
applicableStandards: PCI DSS v4.0 (Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard)
Malaysia PDPA 2010 (Act 709) -- customer personal data protection
CSA Cloud Controls Matrix v4 (CCM v4)
BNM e-money guidelines (for digital wallet operators)

pciDssCloudRequirements.description: PCI DSS v4.0 applies to all entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data regardless of whether infrastructure is on-premises or cloud-hosted. Cloud deployment shifts some PCI responsibilities to the CSP but the merchant/service provider retains accountability.

sharedResponsibility.description: PCI DSS shared responsibility in cloud varies by service model. The merchant is always responsible for compliance -- CSP compliance (via AOC/SOC) supports but does not replace the merchant's obligation.

iaaS.customerResponsibility: OS patching, application security, access control, encryption, logging, network segmentation, vulnerability management

paaS.cspResponsibility: Physical security, hypervisor, OS, runtime, middleware patching
paaS.customerResponsibility: Application security, access control, data encryption, logging configuration, cardholder data handling

saaS.customerResponsibility: Access management, data classification, monitoring of SaaS security configuration, vendor due diligence

requirements.cloudImplication: Cloud network segmentation using VPCs, subnets, security groups, and NACLs must isolate the Cardholder Data Environment (CDE) from other cloud workloads. Cloud-native firewalls and WAF replace traditional network firewalls.
implementation: Dedicated VPC/VNET for CDE workloads -- no co-hosting with non-PCI workloads
Security groups restricting CDE access to only necessary ports and sources
WAF deployed for all internet-facing payment endpoints
VPC flow logs enabled and monitored for CDE network segments
Micro-segmentation between CDE tiers (web, application, database)

requirements.cloudImplication: Cardholder data stored in cloud must be encrypted with strong cryptography. Customer-managed encryption keys (CMEK) recommended. Tokenisation preferred over storing actual card data in cloud.
implementation: Tokenise payment card data at point of capture -- store tokens, not card numbers, in cloud
If card data must be stored: encrypt with AES-256 using CMEK
Implement key management in HSM (CloudHSM, Azure Dedicated HSM, Cloud HSM)
Automated discovery and classification of cardholder data in cloud storage
Data retention policies with automated secure deletion for expired card data

requirements.cloudImplication: All transmission of cardholder data over cloud networks must use TLS 1.2 or higher. Internal service-to-service communication within the CDE must also be encrypted.
implementation: TLS 1.2+ enforced on all load balancers and API gateways handling payment data
Internal service mesh with mTLS for CDE microservices
HSTS headers on all payment-facing web applications
Certificate management with automated renewal
Disable TLS 1.0 and 1.1 on all endpoints

requirements.pciRequirement: Requirement 6: Develop and Maintain Secure Systems
requirements.cloudImplication: Cloud-deployed payment applications must follow secure development lifecycle. Container images and IaC templates must be security-tested. Cloud-native vulnerability scanning must cover all CDE components.
implementation: SAST and DAST in CI/CD pipeline for payment applications
Container image vulnerability scanning before deployment to CDE
IaC security scanning (tfsec, Checkov, cfn-lint) for CDE infrastructure templates
Automated patching for CDE cloud instances and managed services
Web application firewall rules aligned with OWASP Top 10

requirements.pciRequirement: Requirement 10: Log and Monitor All Access
requirements.cloudImplication: Cloud audit logging must capture all access to cardholder data and CDE resources. Logs must be centralised, tamper-protected, and retained for minimum 12 months (3 months immediately available).
implementation: CloudTrail/Activity Log/Audit Log enabled for all CDE accounts
Application-level logging for all cardholder data access
Centralised SIEM integration with PCI-specific detection rules
Immutable log storage with tamper protection
Daily log review and automated alerting for suspicious activity
Minimum 12-month log retention with 3 months immediately accessible

customerDataPrivacy.description: E-commerce platforms process large volumes of customer personal data including names, addresses, phone numbers, email, purchase history, and browsing behaviour. Malaysia PDPA 2010 applies to all customer data processing.

requirements.name: Customer consent and purpose limitation
requirements.description: Explicit consent required for processing customer personal data. Data may only be used for the stated purpose. Marketing use requires separate consent. PDPA s6 and s6(1) apply.

requirements.name: Customer data minimisation in cloud
requirements.description: Collect and store only necessary customer data. Anonymise or pseudonymise data used for analytics. Implement data retention policies with automated deletion of expired data.

requirements.name: Customer data breach notification
requirements.description: If customer data is breached from cloud infrastructure, PDPA notification obligations apply. E-commerce platforms should have pre-prepared breach notification procedures.

requirements.name: Cross-border customer data transfer
requirements.description: E-commerce platforms operating across ASEAN may need to transfer customer data across borders. PDPA s129 cross-border transfer provisions apply. CSP data replication settings must align with permitted jurisdictions.

requirements.name: Customer data access and deletion rights
requirements.description: Customers have the right to access their personal data (PDPA s12) and request correction. E-commerce platforms must implement data retrieval and deletion capabilities for cloud-hosted customer data.

keyRequirements.name: PCI DSS cloud compliance programme
keyRequirements.description: E-commerce platforms handling payment card data must achieve and maintain PCI DSS compliance in their cloud environment. CSP PCI AOC covers CSP-side responsibilities only.
implementation: Obtain CSP's PCI DSS AOC (Attestation of Compliance) for IaaS/PaaS infrastructure
Map PCI DSS requirements to cloud-specific controls (see PCI Cloud Computing Guidelines)
Implement dedicated CDE in isolated cloud VPC/VNET
Annual PCI DSS assessment covering cloud infrastructure by QSA
Quarterly ASV scans of CDE cloud resources
Penetration testing of CDE cloud environment (annual + after significant changes)

keyRequirements.name: API security for payment and customer interfaces
keyRequirements.description: E-commerce APIs (payment processing, customer data, order management) must be secured against API abuse, injection, and unauthorized access.
implementation: OAuth 2.0 with short-lived tokens for API authentication
API rate limiting and throttling to prevent abuse
Input validation and output encoding on all API endpoints
API gateway with request/response inspection
API security testing in CI/CD pipeline
Bot detection and mitigation for automated attacks

keyRequirements.name: Account security and fraud prevention
keyRequirements.description: Protect customer accounts from credential stuffing, account takeover, and fraud. Cloud-based identity and fraud detection services help at scale.
implementation: MFA option for customer accounts (encourage but typically cannot mandate for consumers)
Credential stuffing detection and mitigation (WAF, bot detection)
Account lockout after repeated failed authentication attempts
Anomalous login detection (new device, unusual location, impossible travel)
Fraud detection for payment transactions using ML-based cloud services

keyRequirements.name: DDoS protection for e-commerce availability
keyRequirements.description: E-commerce platforms are high-value DDoS targets, especially during peak shopping events (sales, holidays). Cloud-native DDoS protection is essential.
implementation: Enable CSP DDoS protection (Shield, DDoS Protection, Cloud Armor)
CDN distribution for static content to absorb volumetric attacks
Auto-scaling configured to handle legitimate traffic spikes
DDoS response playbook with CSP escalation procedures
Regular load testing to validate DDoS resilience

pdpaCompliance.description: PDPA 2010 compliance specific to e-commerce cloud operations.
obligations: Register as data user with PDPA Commissioner if processing personal data commercially
Display privacy notice clearly on e-commerce platform
Obtain consent before processing customer personal data
Implement security measures proportionate to the sensitivity and volume of data (s9 PDPA)
Respond to data subject access requests (s12 PDPA) for cloud-hosted data
Comply with cross-border transfer restrictions (s129 PDPA) for international operations

nacsaObligations.description: NACSA obligations for e-commerce platforms designated as NCII under the Trade, Industry and Economy sector.
nacsaObligations.applicability: Applies to e-commerce platforms and digital services designated as NCII under NACSA Act 854. Major platforms processing critical transaction volumes or citizen data may be designated.
obligations: s18: Cloud-hosted e-commerce NCII must have appropriate cybersecurity measures including PCI DSS compliance and customer data protection
s21: Annual risk assessment covering cloud-hosted e-commerce systems including payment processing and customer databases
s23: Biennial audit including cloud security posture and PCI DSS compliance
s26: 6-hour notification for significant cloud security incidents affecting NCII e-commerce systems

sectorSpecificThreats.threat: Payment card skimming through Magecart-style cloud injection
sectorSpecificThreats.impact: Card data theft; PCI DSS non-compliance; customer financial loss; brand damage

sectorSpecificThreats.threat: Customer database exfiltration from misconfigured cloud storage
sectorSpecificThreats.impact: Mass PII exposure; PDPA breach notification; class action risk; customer trust loss

sectorSpecificThreats.threat: DDoS attack during peak shopping event
sectorSpecificThreats.impact: Revenue loss during peak period; customer experience degradation; competitor advantage

sectorSpecificThreats.threat: API abuse enabling mass account takeover
sectorSpecificThreats.impact: Customer account compromise; fraudulent transactions; customer trust erosion
